approach: Conservative (Approach A): Make the minimum changes required — snapshot the ref value at the top of each cleanup function, and add pendingTimeoutId + cancelPending() to AudioManager. Touch only the three source files and their corresponding test files.

steps.action: In the useEffect cleanup at line 68-70, snapshot timeoutsRef.current before clearing: `const ids = timeoutsRef.current; ids.forEach(clearTimeout); timeoutsRef.current = [];`
In the useEffect cleanup at line 114-116, apply the same snapshot fix: `const ids = timeoutsRef.current; ids.forEach(clearTimeout); timeoutsRef.current = [];`

Add `private pendingTimeoutId: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null;` as a class instance field. Add public `cancelPending()` method. At the start of `playWord()`, call `this.cancelPending()`. Replace the anonymous `setTimeout(...)` on line 59 with `this.pendingTimeoutId = setTimeout(...)`.

Add test: 'clears timeouts on unmount (no stale-ref leak)'. Render, trigger a tap that schedules a setTimeout, then unmount inside act(). Use jest.spyOn(global, 'clearTimeout') to verify clearTimeout was called.
Add test: 'clears pending timeouts on unmount'. Flip two non-matching cards (schedules 400ms + 800ms timeouts), then unmount — verify no pending timers cause post-unmount state updates.
Add describe block for `cancelPending()`: test it clears a pending timeout; test that calling playWord() a second time before the first timeout fires cancels the first one.

expected_tests: ListenAndFind — clears timeouts on unmount (no stale-ref leak)
MatchingPairs — clears pending timeouts on unmount
AudioManager — cancelPending — clears pending timeout if one exists
AudioManager — cancelPending — is a no-op when no pending timeout
AudioManager — playWord — cancels previous pending timeout on second call
risk_areas: The timeoutsRef.current reset (`= []`) in cleanup must not interfere with ongoing renders — safe because cleanup only runs on unmount (deps=[]).
audio.ts cancelPending only cancels the inter-word delay setTimeout, not the Howl playback itself. This is intentional per the task spec.
The existing audio.test.ts uses `freshAudioManager()` which re-requires the module — new tests must follow this same pattern to avoid singleton state pollution.

MatchingPairs has a nested setTimeout inside the setCards callback (line 91: setTimeout(() => checkComplete(updated), 0)) — this is NOT pushed to timeoutsRef and is intentionally a zero-delay flush, so it does not need fixing.
persistence_strategy: N/A — this task makes no data storage changes. All changes are in-memory timer management within React component lifecycles.
error_handling_approach: N/A — no new external calls or async operations are introduced. Existing error handling is unchanged.
